channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trudeau to cut sales tax and send checks to millions of Canadians as election looms
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau 's government announced plans Thursday to temporarily lift the federal sales tax off a number of items and send checks to millions of Canadians who are dealing with rising costs and as a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korean leader says past diplomacy only confirmed US hostility
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said his past negotiations with the United States only confirmed Washington’s “unchangeable” hostility toward his country and described his nuclear buildup as the only way to counter external threats, state media said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Netanyahu ‘in very bad company’ with ICC indictment: William Cohen
item.description: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and a former defense minister on war crimes allegations.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Russia tests ballistic missile in Ukraine strike
item.description: After launching a powerful intermediate range ballistic missile toward Ukraine overnight Russian president Vladimir Putin is threatening more aggressive strikes against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: ICC issues warrant for Israel's Netanyahu as Gaza death toll soars past 44,000
item.description: The International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants on Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and a Hamas military leader, accusing them of war crimes and crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Warrants put Israeli PM and others in a small group of leaders accused of crimes against humanity
item.description: Arrest warrants issued for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and a Hamas leader put them in a small group of leaders to be accused of crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UN nuclear agency’s board condemns Iran for the 2nd time this year for failing to fully cooperate
item.description: The U.N. nuclear watchdog’s board has condemned Iran for failing to cooperate fully with the agency, the second time it has done so in just five months.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Liechtenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Putin touts Russia's new missile and delivers a menacing warning to NATO
item.description: The new ballistic missile fired by Russia struck a military-industrial facility in the central Ukrainian city of Dnipro, but its real mission was delivering a deadly new message to NATO.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A draft deal on climate money for developing world is silent on how much as UN summit nears end
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — Countries of the world took turns rejecting a new but vague draft text released early Thursday which attempts to form the spine of any deal reached at United Nations climate talks on money for developing countries to transition to clean energy and adapt to climate change.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, SIBI ARASU, MICHAEL PHILLIS and MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US citizen among 4 dead in Laos after suspected alcohol poisoning
item.description: Four people have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos after officials said that multiple people had been sickened in town popular with backpackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu office rips ICC after warrant issued
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office said in a statement that Israel "utterly rejects" the International Criminal Court's (ICC) arrest warrants issued Thursday for Netanyahu, former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant and a senior Hamas official for allegations related to war crimes from the
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: A former staffer exposes how Russia's disinformation machine worked in Central African Republic
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — When Ephrem Yalike-Ngonzo was first approached in 2019 by a Russian who suggested he help promote the activities of the Central African Republic's army and Russian forces in the country, the journalist believed that he was doing the right thing.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC rejects Israeli appeals, issues arrest warrants for Benjamin Netanyahu, Yoav Gallant
item.description: The U.N.'s International Criminal Court approved arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-UK Deputy Prime Minister John Prescott is hailed as a working-class hero after his death at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British politician John Prescott, a pugnacious and personable former merchant seaman who rose to the post of deputy prime minister in Tony Blair's Labour government, has died at age 86. Prescott’s family announced his death on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: The world’s top war-crimes court has issued arrest warrants for the leaders of Israel and Hamas, including Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ursula Haverbeck, German far-right activist repeatedly convicted for Holocaust denial, dies at 96
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Ursula Haverbeck, a prominent German far-right activist who accumulated a string of convictions for denying the Holocaust, has died. She was 96. Haverbeck died on Wednesday, her lawyer Wolfram Nahrath told German news agency dpa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Russia attacked Ukraine with a new missile that he claims the West can't stop
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says Moscow has tested a new intermediate-range missile in a strike on Ukraine and that it could use the weapon against countries that have allowed Kyiv to use their missiles to strike Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Volcano on Iceland's Reykjanes Peninsula erupts for the 7th time in a year
item.description: A volcano on the Reykjanes Peninsula in southwestern Iceland is spewing lava from a fissure in its seventh eruption since December.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Zealand police begin arrests for gang symbol ban as new law takes effect
item.description: A ban on New Zealanders wearing or displaying symbols of gang affiliation in public has taken effect, with police officers arresting a man for breaking the law three minutes later.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: About 1,500 migrants form a new caravan in Mexico. Here's what it means
item.description: TAPACHULA, Mexico (AP) — About 1,500 migrants formed a new caravan Wednesday in southern Mexico, hoping to walk or catch rides to the U.S. border. The migrants are mainly from Central and South America.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis rejects tradition, opts for humble wooden casket and burial outside Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis announced this week that he intends on having a much more simple funeral than those of his predecessors at the Vatican, including a humble wooden casket.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chief opponent to Uganda's president appears in court days after going missing
item.description: Kizza Besigye, a prominent critic of Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni, appeared in a military court Wednesday after being reported missing in Kenya.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan says it will watch China’s military activity after Beijing admits violating Japanese airspace
item.description: Japanese officials said they are watching to see if China prevents further airspace violations; China explained that an August violation of Japanese airspace was unintentional.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy answers whether he's willing to cede Crimea, other territory in peace deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy tells Fox News that his country "cannot legally acknowledge any occupied territory of Ukraine as Russian.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sweden, Finland and Norway release new advice on surviving war amid concerns over Russia, Ukraine escalation
item.description: Sweden, Finland and Norway this week released detailed guidance for citizens on how to survive a crisis situation like extreme weather or war-like conditions that could hamper food or internet access.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1,000 days of war in Ukraine as Zelenskyy doubles down on aerial options with ATACMS, drones and missiles
item.description: Ukraine marks 1,000 days of war with Russia on Tuesday as President Zelenskyy eyes plan to develop thousands of long-range drones and military targets inside Russia using US-supplied ATACMS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation
